mnemonics: FANCY - Functionality As Need Can Yield Answers
keyPoints: Identify the problem statement and the goals of the software system.
List all the inputs, processing, and outputs of the system to derive functional requirements.
Nonfunctional requirements include performance, security, usability, and maintainability.
Analyze user needs and preferences to derive nonfunctional requirements.
Consider the technical constraints of the system, such as hardware and software limitations.
Prioritize requirements based on their importance and feasibility.
Create use cases and user stories to validate functional and nonfunctional requirements.
Document all requirements clearly and concisely for future reference.
